problem: Replace an aging Squarespace presence with a high-performance custom website for a design studio: portfolio, blog, contact form, and dark/light mode, targeting Lighthouse 100 across all four categories.
primaryUser: Prospective clients (creative directors, marketing leads); managed by one in-house designer/content editor
successCriteria: Lighthouse 100 on performance, SEO, accessibility, best practices; < 50ms LCP on 4G; CMS-editable content without code changes; deployed to Vercel with preview deployments on each PR.

stories.title: Design System & Tokens
stories.description: CSS custom properties for colour, spacing, typography in dark/light modes; Figma token export pipeline; Storybook with all base components.

stories.title: Portfolio Section
stories.description: Filterable grid of case studies; each case study is a MDX file with a hero image, tags, summary, and full write-up; loading skeleton + lazy images via Intersection Observer.

stories.title: Blog
stories.description: MDX-powered blog with category filtering, related posts, reading time estimate, syntax highlighting, and RSS feed.

stories.title: Contact & SEO
stories.description: Contact form with server-side validation + Resend email delivery; robots.txt; XML sitemap auto-generated from routes; Open Graph meta tags for every page.

stories.title: Performance & Deployment
stories.description: Next.js 14 App Router with ISR; image optimisation pipeline; Vercel deployment configuration; Lighthouse CI gate in GitHub Actions preventing merge on regression.

stackConstraints: Next.js 14 App Router, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, MDX, Vercel hosting
openQuestions: Will the designer provide Figma tokens export or are we building the token set from scratch?
Should the contact form include a CAPTCHA in v1?

modelRecommendation.estimatedCostNote: Estimated $0.60–$1.00 — frontend-only project, smaller context budget
